primary_detection.name: Underscore Prefix Obfuscation
primary_detection.description: Uses varying numbers of underscores as variable names to create visual noise
evidence: Progressive underscore counts up to 27+ characters
Function names also use underscore patterns
Makes code nearly impossible to read or review

underlying_threats.name: Privileged File Access
underlying_threats.description: Attempts to read /etc/shadow which requires root privileges

underlying_threats.name: Auto-Execution on Import
underlying_threats.description: Main function called at module load

obfuscation_techniques.effectiveness: High - very difficult to track variable usage

obfuscation_techniques.purpose: Build commands dynamically to avoid detection
Variables look similar, hard to distinguish

verdict_reasoning: Underscore-based obfuscation is an advanced technique specifically designed to defeat code review. The targeting of shadow file and credentials confirms malicious intent for credential harvesting.
recommended_actions: Flag code with repetitive underscore variable patterns
Add heuristic for underscore-only variable names
